evidence: ExplorationHandlers private helpers parseRelationConfig and parseExplorationParams cleanly separate HTTP parsing from business logic
SemanticFieldExplorer facade delegates single-seed, multi-seed, and comparison to dedicated algorithm classes — each with a single focused responsibility
SketchHandlers.executeRelationQueries abstracts the loop-over-relations with a BiFunction builder callback, removing two near-identical loops

AdjectiveProfile record takes 10 positional constructor arguments (adjective, nounScores, presentInCount, totalNouns, avgLogDice, maxLogDice, minLogDice, variance, commonalityScore, distinctivenessScore) — at the call site in buildAdjectiveProfileList, seven of them are double literals with no label
buildCoreExploreResponse in ExplorationHandlers accepts 'Map<String, Object> extraParams' for open-ended response augmentation — currently used for only one key (nouns_per)

strengths: SemanticFieldExplorer correctly separates three different algorithm strategies (exploreByPattern, exploreMultiSeed, compareCollocateProfiles) into focused classes, with itself acting as a stable facade
ExplorationHandlers.parseExplorationParams and parseRelationConfig are precisely scoped helpers that do exactly one thing each
SketchHandlers.executeRelationQueries BiFunction builder pattern eliminates duplicated loop boilerplate across surface and dependency sketch paths
HttpApiUtils Javadoc explicitly justifies co-locating request parsing with response writing, signalling a deliberate design decision rather than an oversight
CollocateQueryHelper's two-phase pipeline (collectHits → scoreHits) with a named HitRecord intermediate type is a proportionate decomposition
design_coherence.issue_character: Two design issues in different subsystems — one is a wide positional constructor that makes construction sites opaque, the other is an open-ended Map parameter that defers typing indefinitely. Neither is systemic but both represent patterns that grow harder to audit as the codebase evolves.
design_coherence.score_rationale: Design coherence is strong in the exploration and query layers. Functions are focused, the algorithm decomposition is well-structured, and the facade pattern in SemanticFieldExplorer cleanly encapsulates complexity. The two defects (wide AdjectiveProfile constructor, open extraParams Map) are real costs to a developer modifying CollocateProfileComparator or adding a new explore parameter. They are not systemic but they are in high-traffic paths. This sits at 80 (solid but recurring friction): a developer mostly succeeds but hits one or two spots where they must count positional arguments or guess the Map contract.

issues.summary: AdjectiveProfile record has 10 positional double parameters making construction sites unreadable
evidence: AdjectiveProfile record: (adjective, nounScores, presentInCount, totalNouns, avgLogDice, maxLogDice, minLogDice, variance, commonalityScore, distinctivenessScore) — 10 components

Call site in buildAdjectiveProfileList: 'profiles.add(new AdjectiveProfile(adj, fullScores, presentIn, nounCount, avgScore, maxScore, minScore, variance, commonalityScore, distinctivenessScore))' — 7 positional double arguments with no names at the call site
A reviewer must count positions to verify that avgScore maps to avgLogDice and not maxLogDice; minScore and maxScore are adjacent doubles that are trivially transposable
issues.suggestion: Introduce a static factory or builder to group the computed statistics. The cleanest option is a nested AdjectiveStats record: 'record AdjectiveStats(int presentInCount, int totalNouns, double avgLogDice, double maxLogDice, double minLogDice, double variance, double commonalityScore, double distinctivenessScore)' and change AdjectiveProfile to '(String adjective, Map<String,Double> nounScores, AdjectiveStats stats)'. Update buildAdjectiveProfileList to build AdjectiveStats inline before constructing AdjectiveProfile.

issues.summary: buildCoreExploreResponse accepts an open Map<String,Object> extraParams with no type contract
evidence: Signature: 'private Map<String,Object> buildCoreExploreResponse(String relationType, int topCollocates, int minShared, double minLogDice, Map<String,Object> extraParams)'
Currently called with: 'Map.of("nouns_per", exploreParams.nounsPerCollocate())' from single-seed path and 'Map.of()' from multi-seed path
The open Map parameter means any future caller can inject arbitrary keys into the parameters sub-map without the method knowing or validating them
issues.suggestion: Remove the extraParams Map and instead add a nullable Integer nounsPerCollocate parameter (or use Optional<Integer>): 'buildCoreExploreResponse(String relationType, int topCollocates, int minShared, double minLogDice, @Nullable Integer nounsPerCollocate)'. Inside the method: 'if (nounsPerCollocate != null) paramsUsed.put("nouns_per", nounsPerCollocate)'. This makes the type contract explicit and closes the open-ended extension point.

root_causes: AdjectiveProfile was designed as a data container for all computed statistics simultaneously; grouping the stats fields was deferred
extraParams Map was a quick way to handle the single-seed-only nouns_per parameter without changing the method signature
